name: baoyu-image-gen
description: AI image generation with OpenAI and Google APIs. Supports text-to-image, reference images, aspect ratios, and parallel generation (recommended 4 concurrent subagents). Use when user asks to generate, create, or draw images.
capabilities: Generate images using OpenAI (DALL-E 3, GPT-Image-1.5) or Google (Gemini, Imagen) APIs
Use reference images for style transfer (Google multimodal models only)
Generate multiple images in parallel using background subagents
Read prompts from files for complex generation workflows
author: jimliu
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 183 pattern matches, but all evaluated as false positives for this legitimate image generation skill. Dynamic imports load internal provider modules (google.ts, openai.ts). Environment access reads standard API keys (OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY). Network calls are to legitimate AI endpoints (api.openai.com, generativelanguage.googleapis.com). Filesystem operations handle prompt files and save generated images. No malicious intent detected.
valueStatement: Create high-quality AI-generated images without leaving your development environment. Supports multiple providers, aspect ratios, and reference images for precise visual control.
userTitle: Generate AI Images with OpenAI and Google
limitations: Requires valid API keys for OpenAI or Google
Reference images only supported with Google multimodal models
Maximum recommended concurrency is 8 subagents for batch generation
Image generation costs apply based on provider pricing

useCases.title: Content Creation
useCases.description: Generate blog post featured images, social media graphics, and marketing visuals with consistent aspect ratios and quality settings.

useCases.title: UI/UX Design
useCases.description: Create mockup images, illustration assets, and design concepts for web and mobile applications.

useCases.title: Batch Asset Generation
useCases.description: Generate multiple images in parallel for presentations, comics, or infographic series using concurrent subagents.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Image Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate an image of a serene mountain landscape at sunset with snow-capped peaks reflected in a calm lake

promptTemplates.title: Social Media Graphic
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a 16:9 banner image for a tech conference with abstract geometric shapes in blue and purple gradients, including space for text overlay

promptTemplates.title: Batch Illustration Set
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate 4 images in parallel for a children's book: 1) a friendly robot, 2) a magical forest, 3) a flying car, 4) an underwater city. Use consistent art style across all images.

promptTemplates.title: Style Reference Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate an image of a futuristic cityscape using reference.png as the style guide. Match the color palette and artistic style of the reference.
promptTemplates.scenario: Using reference images for style transfer

bestPractices: Use --quality 2k for final assets and --quality normal for quick previews to manage costs
Leverage parallel generation with 4 concurrent subagents when creating multiple images
Store API keys in ~/.baoyu-skills/.env for secure, persistent configuration
antiPatterns: Do not commit API keys to version control - use environment files instead
Avoid generating images sequentially when batch processing - use parallel subagents
Do not use reference images with OpenAI provider - they are only supported with Google multimodal models

faq.question: Which API provider should I use?
faq.answer: Google (Gemini/Imagen) is the default and supports reference images and 4K output. Use OpenAI if you prefer DALL-E 3 or have existing OpenAI credits.

faq.question: How do I set up my API keys?
faq.answer: Create a file at ~/.baoyu-skills/.env with your keys: OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... or GOOGLE_API_KEY=... The skill will automatically load them on each run.

faq.question: Can I use reference images to control the style?
faq.answer: Yes, but only with Google multimodal models (gemini-3-pro-image-preview or gemini-3-flash-preview). Use the --ref flag followed by image file paths.

faq.question: What is the recommended way to generate multiple images?
faq.answer: Use background subagents with run_in_background=true for parallel generation. The recommended concurrency is 4 subagents, with a maximum of 8.

faq.question: How do I specify custom image dimensions?
faq.answer: Use --ar for aspect ratios (16:9, 1:1, 4:3, etc.) or --size for exact dimensions (1024x1024). Aspect ratio is recommended for most use cases.

faq.question: What happens if image generation fails?
faq.answer: The skill automatically retries failed generations once before reporting an error. Check your API key and network connection if issues persist.
